Modern social-first product photography with tactile editorial energy, SCARLETT wearing a linen shirt seated at a white vanity before her microphone, mid-reach with hand lifting the lid of a matte white box on the surface, body tensed with potential energy and gaze off-camera, bright window light slashing across clean minimal studio surfaces with crisp white tones and subtle reflections, shallow depth of field emphasizing the immediate foreground action against a softly blurred background, high-key natural daylight with sharp directional shadows and a single punchy highlight catching the emerging product surface, eye-level locked framing with subtle anamorphic lens character and clean true-to-life color grading.

  <reference-images>
    - Image 1 [CHARACTER REF]: SCARLETT - Match the real-world appearance of Sienna Blake exactly
    - Image 2 [LOCATION REF]: INT. BONDI STUDIO - MORNING - White vanity illuminated by bright window light
    
    IMPORTANT: Reference images CARRY the visual identity of their labeled objects. When the prompt names an UPPERCASE element token, render it faithfully from its ELEMENT REF image — do not generate a new version from the prose. When the prompt names a LOCATION REF, use that image for the environment, lighting, and architectural identity. Prose describes how things are framed, lit, and positioned in the shot — it does not redefine what the reference already shows.
  </reference-images>
